title: poa hothamensis
intro: Poa hothamensis, also known as Hotham's grass or Alpine poa, is a species of grass native to the alpine regions of Tasmania and Victoria in Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. Poa hothamensis can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
